name: SPX Deletion-Reversal: forced-seller rebound in demoted-but-solvent names
mechanism: When S&P DJI deletes a stock from the S&P 500 for non-M&A reasons (demotion), ~$15T of indexed and benchmark-hugging capital must sell within days of the effective date regardless of fundamentals â€” a large, information-FREE supply shock. Demoted names are simultaneously momentum losers (institutions window-dress them away) and lose the perpetual passive bid, so the price pressure overshoots fair value; the classic literature (Harris-Gurel 1986; Chen-Noronha-Singal 2004) documents multi-month post-deletion reversal. The catalyst-conditioned twist that makes this a factor-on-catalyst rather than a naive event: condition entry on an EDGAR-PIT solvency/value floor (positive TTM EPS or positive FCF as of entry) to amputate the fat left tail of true corporate deaths â€” the exact failure mode that killed the distressed-insider study (mean negative once delistings counted). We buy forced-seller overshoot, not distress.
why_generalizes: The mechanism is structural, not fitted: mandated passive flows at a published effective date, and passive AUM share has only grown 2018-2026. It was documented academically decades before our sample window, on data we never touched, so it cannot be an artifact of our composite's data mining. It requires no parameter the literature didn't already fix (sell pressure peaks at effective date; reversal plays out over 1-6 months). Honest counter-evidence stated up front: Greenwood-Sammon (2022, "The Disappearing Index Effect") shows announcement-day index effects have shrunk toward zero in recent decades â€” which is exactly why the survival prior below is low and the bar is pre-registered, not why the test should be skipped: their result is strongest for ADDITIONS (arbed by index-arb desks); the slower multi-month deletion REVERSAL in small, shunned, momentum-loser names is the less-arbed corner, and our solvency filter is novel relative to the published constructions.
data_sources: data/universe/sp500_changes.csv â€” Wikipedia add/remove log with EFFECTIVE dates (already ingested via scripts/fetch_sp500_membership; 159 removals 2018-2026, verified this session)

Polygon delisting-inclusive daily OHLCV, FROZEN in existing sp500_pit snapshots â€” build_snapshot.py freezes FULL-window membership incl. removals (build_snapshot.py:133-156), so demoted names' post-removal prices are already in the panels
EDGAR PIT fundamentals (fundamentals_pit.json per snapshot, filing-date gated) for the solvency/value floor
SPY series from the same snapshots for pre-event beta / Jensen alpha
pit_plan: Event date = EFFECTIVE removal date from sp500_changes.csv (announcement precedes effective by ~5 trading days; entering AFTER effective guarantees the event is public and the forced selling has occurred). Entry as_of = effective date + 5 trading days; entry price = that day's close. Demotion-vs-M&A filter is PIT-safe by construction: an event qualifies iff the ticker still prints a Polygon bar on the entry day (observable at entry; acquired names have stopped trading). Solvency/value floor reads fundamentals_pit.json with filing_date <= entry as_of (the existing loader's gate). Pre-event beta = 252d OLS vs SPY using only prices <= entry as_of. Exit at entry+126 trading days or last traded bar if delisted mid-hold (delisting-aware, realize final price â€” insider_capitulation_study template behavior). Every event sourced from frozen snapshot price panels, never live fetches; events restricted to the first 12 months of each 2yr snapshot so the 126d hold never runs off the panel edge (consecutive snapshots overlap by 1yr, so coverage is complete).
entry_signal: Ticker removed from S&P 500 (action=remove in changes log) AND still trading 5 sessions after the effective date (= demotion, not acquisition) AND passes EDGAR-PIT solvency floor (TTM EPS > 0 OR FCF > 0 as of entry). Buy at close of effective-date + 5 trading days.
horizon: 126 trading days (~6 months) per event; ~15-20 raw events/yr, est. 50-80 qualifying demotions 2018-2026 after the M&A + solvency filters (pre-registered minimum N=50, else ABORT as underpowered rather than report).
universe: Ex-S&P-500 demotions only â€” names exiting the index but still listed (mid/small-cap by then). Deliberately disjoint from the live S&P 500 production book, which also makes it incapable of contaminating the frozen forward-paper run.
construction: Long-only equal-weight event book: each qualifying event gets 1/N slot, overlapping holds netted into a daily portfolio, $100M notional (ledger rule â€” $10k rounding dampens), cost arms at 25bps and 50bps round-trip. Graded on event-level Jensen alpha (pre-event 252d OLS beta vs SPY) and book-level CAPM alpha â€” never raw return. Matched controls per event: 3 same-sector S&P names nearest in size and 12-1 momentum that were NOT removed (the insider_capitulation_study matched-control + within-year permutation pattern, which is the reusable gold-standard event harness per the ledger).
orthogonality: Anti-momentum by construction: demotions are 12-1 momentum LOSERS, so expected correlation to the momentum/momval books is near zero to negative. Pre-registered checks: (a) median 12-1 momentum percentile of entered names vs the contemporaneous S&P cross-section must be < 30th pct (confirms we are buying what the momentum book shuns â€” not a clone); (b) monthly book-return correlation vs the momval forward book and vs the production composite each < 0.3. Vs value: mild positive tilt expected (we buy beaten-down solvent names) â€” acceptable; the catalyst timing, not the value level, is the claimed edge, and the matched controls are momentum/size-matched precisely to strip the generic loser-reversal/value effect.
preregistered_bar: Fixed BEFORE any return is computed, fail-any-kill, no post-hoc re-tuning (entry lag 5d and hold 126d are locked; a 63/189d hold row is REPORTED for decay context but not graded): (1) N >= 50 qualifying events, else ABORT-underpowered (not a pass or fail). (2) Median event-level 126d CAPM-alpha >= +4% AND mean > 0 with delisted events included at realized loss. (3) Beats matched controls by >= +3pp median alpha with within-year permutation p < 0.05. (4) Win rate vs own matched control >= 55%. (5) Breadth: positive median event alpha in >= 6 of 8 calendar years 2018-2025 (the event-study analogue of the >=6/7-windows + >=60%-of-phases bar; events are date-pinned so rebalance-phase noise does not apply, calendar-year breadth replaces it). (6) Survives 50bps round-trip cost arm on all of the above. (7) Orthogonality gates (a) and (b) above. (8) Zero price-artifact stitches in the event panel after scan (any hit -> drop ticker via the shipped guard, rerun).
gauntlet_commands: uv run python -m scripts.fetch_sp500_membership  # refresh data/universe/sp500_changes.csv (the event source) to current

# NEW SCRIPT (one build): scripts/research/spx_deletion_reversal_study.py â€” direct clone of scripts/insider_capitulation_study.py (the ledger's explicitly-reusable event-study template: PIT entry gate, pre-event beta, delisting-aware returns, matched controls, within-year permutation, pre-registered ship_rule JSON in the docstring BEFORE running) with the event source swapped from Form-4 clusters to sp500_changes.csv removals + the solvency floor from fundamentals_pit.json

uv run python scripts/research/spx_deletion_reversal_study.py --snapshot-ids acd1e7401c6484cf a36c9bfd0c353b53 2c853f10c6638fc0 57016c1293f136cd 1c1c314850bb7368 a347da6750f42939 fe045eff04a15142 --changes data/universe/sp500_changes.csv --entry-lag-days 5 --hold-days 126 --solvency-floor eps_or_fcf_positive --min-events 50 --controls 3 --match sector,size,mom_12_1 --notional 100000000 --cost-bps 50 --report-holds 63,189 --output reports/spx_deletion_reversal_2018_2026.json

uv run python -m scripts.research.price_artifact_scan --tickers-file reports/spx_deletion_event_tickers.txt --start 2018-01-01 --end 2026-06-01  # demoted names are EXACTLY the ticker-reuse/stitch population (META lesson); any hit -> drop via src/factors/price_quality.drop_price_artifacts and rerun

# orthogonality verification emitted by the study itself into the output JSON: median mom_12_1 percentile of entries (<30th pct gate) + monthly book-return corr vs reports/trend_forward_paper_momval_state.json equity series (<0.3 gate)
already_tried_check: Cross-referenced both ledger blocks: NOTHING touches index reconstitution â€” no addition, deletion, or rebalance-flow idea appears in tried, killed_families, or unknown_verdicts. Closest relatives and how this differs: (a) distressed-insider capitulation event study (NULL) â€” same harness, different mechanism (insider information vs mandated passive flow); its two fatal lessons (delisting fat tail, sparse 66-ticker coverage) are designed against here via the solvency floor and the verified 159-event count; (b) broad-universe breadth (KILLED) â€” that was a universe-composition lever for the composite, not an event signal; (c) 'regime/market timing' kill family â€” inapplicable, this times nothing market-wide; (d) news-sentiment 63d-cadence kill â€” inapplicable, this is an event book with its own cadence. The membership changes log has been used only as a universe ORACLE, never as a SIGNAL â€” genuinely virgin data already sitting in the repo. Honest risk acknowledged: Greenwood-Sammon-style evidence that index effects have decayed makes this a documented-but-possibly-arbed anomaly, hence the low prior below â€” but it is novel TO THIS LEDGER and costs one cloned script to grade.
novelty: First use of the in-repo PIT membership changes log as an alpha signal rather than a universe filter; first index-reconstitution test in the project; the solvency-floor conditioning (factor-on-catalyst) is not the published academic construction, and the matched-control design (sector+size+momentum-matched non-deleted names) isolates the deletion CATALYST from the generic loser-reversal effect â€” a control the classic papers mostly lack.

name: SP500 Deletion-Pressure Reversal (forced-indexer-flow event study)
mechanism: When S&P DJI deletes a stock for market-cap decline, ~$13T of indexed AUM must sell the entire position by the effective-date close â€” often 10-25% of float crossing the closing auction with ZERO fundamental information content. The seller is price-insensitive by mandate. Post-event, the name is institutionally shunned (index-eligibility mandates, loser-stock neglect), so the dislocation corrects slowly. Limits-to-arbitrage protect this side specifically: the arb requires buying a falling, shrinking, newly-benchmark-orphaned name into forced flow â€” exactly what most institutional capital cannot do. Greenwood-Sammon (2022) showed the ADDITION effect was arbed to zero; the DELETION side is the persistent, harder-to-arb half (Chen-Noronha-Singal asymmetry). This is a microstructure/flows edge, not a factor â€” orthogonal to everything in the ledger.
why_generalizes: The causal driver (indexed AUM as % of market) has grown monotonically since 2000 and is still growing â€” the forced-flow shock gets BIGGER over time, not arbed smaller. The behavioral half (mandate-constrained neglect of benchmark orphans) is structural, not statistical. The trade is contrarian/anti-momentum, so it diversifies the house's momentum-heavy books rather than re-expressing them. Key risk to generalization (stated honestly): many deletions are simultaneously ADDED to S&P MidCap 400, partially offsetting the flow â€” the matched-control bar below exists precisely to separate true flow-reversal from generic loser bounce.
data_sources: data/snapshots/<id>/sp500_changes.csv â€” frozen PIT membership change log (effective date, action, ticker, reason); 92 non-M&A deletions 2018-2026 verified
data/snapshots/<id>/ price panel â€” Polygon, delisting-inclusive, includes removed names (build_snapshot.py:135-145 freezes full-window membership)
SPY series inside each snapshot for pre-event beta + Jensen alpha
No new data purchase required â€” this is the one event class our in-house PIT membership oracle uniquely covers
pit_plan: Event timestamp = effective date from sp500_changes.csv (Wikipedia-sourced effective dates; the S&P announcement precedes the effective date by ~5 trading days, so entering AFTER the effective date guarantees every feature is public). Entry = close of effective_date + 1 trading day (also skips the announcement-window front-run, which is not our edge). Pre-event beta = OLS on 252 trading days ending AT the effective date. Matched controls (the loser-reversal null) selected using only trailing data <= entry date: nearest-neighbor on 12-1 trailing return + 63d dollar volume among never-deleted S&P names. Exit = entry + 63 trading days, delisting-aware (if the name stops trading, realize last available price as the return â€” insider_capitulation_study.py template behavior). Exclude events whose reason matches acquisition/merger/take-private (67 of 159; no tradeable forward path). All prices from frozen snapshots â€” zero live fetches.
entry_signal: Stock removed from S&P 500 with reason = market-capitalization change (non-M&A), per the frozen changes log. Buy at the close of the first trading day AFTER the effective date. No other conditioning (pre-registering zero free parameters).
horizon: 63 trading days per event (house cadence); robustness read-outs at 21d and 126d reported but NOT graded.
universe: Ex-S&P-500 deletions only (~11 tradeable events/yr, 92 pooled 2018-2026) â€” these are still ~$5-15B liquid names at deletion, mostly entering S&P MidCap 400, so 50bps is conservative.
construction: Phase 1 (the gauntlet): pure event study â€” per-event 63d beta-adjusted abnormal return (ret minus pre-event-beta x SPY), pooled + per-year, vs matched controls, within-year permutation null â€” exact insider_capitulation_study.py architecture. Phase 2 (only if Phase 1 passes): long-only rolling sleeve holding every deletion < 63d old, equal-weight, $100M notional, graded on CAPM Jensen alpha; ~11 positions/yr turnover so cost drag at 50bps each way is ~0.1%/yr â€” negligible by design.
orthogonality: Negatively correlated with 12-1 momentum BY CONSTRUCTION â€” names deleted for market-cap decline are mechanically bottom-decile momentum. Expected Spearman vs 12-1 momentum strongly negative; zero overlap with momval/composite books (those hold winners). Value loading likely positive (fallen prices), which is why the matched-control increment (matching on trailing return) is a graded clause, not a footnote: the claim is FLOW reversal, not cheap-loser beta.
preregistered_bar: Fixed before computing any return. PASS requires ALL of: (1) N >= 60 usable non-M&A events with >= 45d post-entry prices; (2) median 63d beta-adjusted abnormal return >= +3.0% (insider-study bar); (3) win rate >= 55%; (4) within-year permutation p < 0.05; (5) matched-control increment >= +2.0pp median (kills the generic loser-reversal explanation â€” without this clause the idea is just anti-momentum, which factor_lab already found dead); (6) positive median abnormal return in >= 6 of 9 calendar years 2018-2026 (the >=60%-of-phases analog for an event strategy); (7) entry-jitter sign stability: median stays positive entering at T+1, T+3, and T+5 closes (anti-phase-luck adaptation); (8) clause 2 still holds after 50bps each way. Any single failure = NO-SHIP, log the null, stop.

uv run python -m scripts.research.index_deletion_study --snapshot-id acd1e7401c6484cf --hold 63 --entry-lag 1 --cost-bps 50 --output reports/index_deletion_2018_2020.json

# entry-jitter sign-stability (clause 7): rerun the pooled set with --entry-lag 3 and --entry-lag 5
# ONLY if all 8 clauses pass: sleeve conversion graded via run_factor_backtest CAPM-alpha machinery + scripts/research/breadth_summary.py aggregation across the 7 windows
already_tried_check: Cross-referenced both ledger entries: NO prior work on index reconstitution / addition-deletion flows anywhere (not in tried, killed_families, or unknown_verdict_scripts). Nearest cousins, all distinct: (a) insider_capitulation_study â€” event study on beaten-down names, NULL, but its events were sparse voluntary insider buys; this event is denser, mechanically forced, and flow-driven; (b) factor_lab rev_5/21 short-term reversal â€” NULL as an unconditional cross-sectional factor; this is event-CONDITIONED reversal with a specific non-fundamental seller, and clause 5 (matched-control increment) exists exactly to prove it is not that dead factor in disguise; (c) 'regime timing' kill family does not apply â€” this is cross-sectional/event, no market-timing component. Genuinely novel within this program.
novelty: First use of the in-house PIT S&P membership oracle as an EVENT SOURCE rather than a universe filter â€” the one dataset this shop owns that directly timestamps a forced, information-free institutional flow. Also the program's first flow/microstructure (rather than factor or gate) hypothesis at daily-bar cadence.
